papers.title_en: AC$^2$P$^2$SL: Adaptive Communication-Computation Pipeline Parallel Split Learning over Edge Networks
papers.authors: Chenyu Liu, Zhaoyang Zhang, Zirui Chen, Zhaohui Yang, Chunhui Feng, Tony Q. S. Quek
papers.evidence: 自适应通信计算流水线并行可有效减少训练时间
papers.tldr: 针对无线边缘网络中分裂学习（SL）因计算和通信顺序执行导致训练时间长的问题，提出自适应计算通信流水线并行分裂学习（AC²P²SL）框架。通过微批处理实现细粒度流水线并行，有效重叠通信与计算，显著降低训练延迟。考虑系统约束和UE异构性，联合优化训练时间并提出分割预分配算法，同时设计自适应重分配策略提升动态环境下的鲁棒性。实验表明该方法能有效缩短训练时间并保护数据隐私。
papers.abstract_en: In wireless edge networks, split learning (SL) enables base station (BS) to utilize the distributed data and computing power across user equipments (UEs) to achieve collaborative model training while protecting local data privacy. However, the inherent sequential execution of computation and communication processes in conventional SL usually leads to long training times. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes an adaptive communication-computation pipeline parallel split learning (AC$^2$P$^2$SL) framework. By conceptualizing the communication and computation processes of UEs and the BS as a unified pipeline, AC$^2$P$^2$SL achieves fine-grained pipeline parallelism across multiple micro-batches. Through this approach, effective overlapping of communication and computation is achieved which results in significant reduction of the overall training latency. Moreover, by considering the system constraints in the communication, computation, and storage dimensions as well as the heterogeneity of UEs, we formulate a joint optimization problem to minimize the training time and propose a corresponding split and pre-allocation algorithm to further enhance the pipeline efficiency. Additionally, accounting for the practical dynamic environments for the UEs, we design an adaptive re-allocation strategy to enhance the system resilience. Extensive experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and robustness of AC$^2$P$^2$SL in reducing training time while ensuring data privacy preservation.

papers.title_en: Federated Learning with Energy-Based Structured Probabilistic Inference
papers.authors: Dario Fenoglio, Daniil Kirilenko, Martin Gjoreski, Marc Langheinrich
papers.evidence: 联邦学习中结构化聚合权重优化，提升通信效率
papers.tldr: 联邦学习在客户端数据非独立同分布时，固定或启发式加权规则难以捕捉异质贡献，导致全局模型次优。本文提出基于条件随机场（CRF）的框架，通过一元势函数建模单个客户端可靠性，成对势函数建模客户端间相关性，经结构化概率推理动态生成聚合权重。在非IID数据下，该方法持续优于多个联邦学习基线，显著加快收敛并提升全局模型精度。该工作为异质联邦学习提供了可解释且高效的自适应聚合机制，具有理论价值和实际应用前景。
papers.abstract_en: Federated learning typically aggregates client updates using fixed or heuristic weighting rules, which can be suboptimal when clients have heterogeneous data and varying contributions to the global model. We propose a framework that refines client aggregation weights using Conditional Random Fields (CRFs). Our method defines unary potentials for individual clients and pairwise potentials for all client pairs, allowing the server to model both client-specific reliability and interactions between clients. The resulting CRF inference produces aggregation weights that enable better convergence of the global training objective. Experiments show that, under non-IID heterogeneity, our approach consistently improves performance over well-established federated learning baselines.
